title: Your data and privacy
description: Disconnect platforms, delete workspaces or your whole account, and understand how Vidflow handles your data.
headings: How Google and YouTube data is handled
body: You stay in control of the data Vidflow holds. This page covers disconnecting platforms, deleting content, deleting your account, and how Google and YouTube data is handled. You keep ownership of your content. Vidflow only receives the license it needs to operate the Service on your behalf. # Disconnect a social platform Open Workspace Settings and go to the Connections tab. Under Connected Accounts , disconnect the account. Disconnecting immediately revokes Vidflow's stored access tokens for that platform and removes the associated profile data. # Delete a workspace Open Workspace Settings , then the Danger Zone tab. Two options: Freeze Workspace halts all operations immediately. The workspace is permanently deleted after 90 days unless you unfreeze it. Permanently Delete removes the workspace and all its content: episodes, clips, posts, schedules, and settings. You type the workspace name to confirm, and it cannot be undone. # Delete your entire account Email support@vidflow.io from your account's email address and ask for account deletion. Your account and data are deleted within 30 days. # Request data deletion by email If you no longer have access to your account, or you want all data associated with you removed, email privacy@vidflow.io with the subject line "Data Deletion Request". Send it from the email address on your account and say which account or connected platform the request covers. Vidflow may ask you to verify ownership first. For a verified request, personal

title: Manage your subscription
description: Start a trial, switch plans, update your card, cancel, and request a refund.
headings: Update your card or view invoices
body: Everything billing-related lives on one page in the app, including current pricing for every plan. This article covers starting a plan, switching plans, payment details, cancellation, and refunds. # Open the Billing page Click your avatar in the app. Choose Account settings . Click Manage plan & billing . The Billing & plan page shows your current plan and its status, your usage meters, and the plan catalog with pricing. If you are on a trial, a banner shows how many days remain. # Start a paid plan Every paid plan starts with a 7-day free trial. A card is required to start the trial. On the Billing page, pick a plan. Click Start 7-day trial . Complete checkout. Payment is handled by Stripe, and you can enter a promotion code during checkout. # Switch plans To upgrade or downgrade, click Switch to [plan name] on the plan you want. The change applies to your existing subscription immediately, and prorated charges or credits appear on your next invoice. Switching never creates a second subscription. On the Agency plan you can also adjust extra brand spaces from the plan card and confirm with Update brand spaces . Extra spaces are billed as an add-on quantity on the same subscription. Note Note: if your plan shows a green Complimentary badge, Vidflow granted your account free full access and no payment is needed. # Update your card or view invoices Click Manage billing to open the secure Stripe billing portal. From there you can update your payment method, view past invoices, an

title: Plans and features
description: What each Vidflow plan includes, from monthly allowances to feature access.
body: Vidflow has four plans: Free, Starter, Pro, and Agency. This page is the reference for what each plan includes. For current pricing, open the Billing page in the app. # Plan limits Every allowance below resets monthly. Usage limits and monthly allowances explains how the meters work. Plan Brand spaces Working seats Episodes per month AI generations per month Clip generations per month Connected social accounts Free 1 1 1 20 3 1 Starter 1 1 2 150 30 3 Pro 1 2 8 600 120 10 Agency 1, expandable 10 8 2,500 500 Unlimited How to read the table: Brand spaces are workspaces. One brand space holds one brand's content, connected accounts, and settings. Everyone gets their first workspace, including on the Free plan. Agency owners can purchase additional brand spaces as an add-on. Working seats are admin and brand manager seats per brand space. The workspace owner counts as one seat. Client reviewer seats are free and unlimited on every plan. Episodes per month is a per-brand-space allowance. On Agency, each additional brand space adds its own episode allowance to your account total. AI generations and clip generations are account-wide pools shared across all your brand spaces. Connected social accounts are counted per brand space. # Features by plan Every plan includes the core workflow: upload episodes, generate clips and AI content within your monthly allowances, connect social accounts, and schedule posts. Paid plans add features on top: Starter and up : client approval links. Share

title: Account security
description: Set up two-factor authentication, manage recovery codes, and control where you are signed in.
body: Protect your Vidflow account with two-factor authentication (2FA) and review every device where you are signed in. To open the Security page, click your avatar, choose Account settings , then click Two-factor & active sessions . # How 2FA works in Vidflow Two-factor authentication adds a second step to your sign-in: a 6-digit code from an authenticator app. Important Important: 2FA applies when you sign in with your email and password. If you sign in with Google, that sign-in is protected by your Google account's own security settings instead. # Turn on two-factor authentication Open the Security page. Click Enable two-factor . Scan the QR code with your authenticator app. Enter the 6-digit code from the app to confirm. Save your recovery codes. Copy them or download the .txt file. Important Important: recovery codes are shown once. Each code works one time if you lose access to your authenticator app. Store them somewhere safe. After setup, signing in with your password prompts for a 6-digit code. You can enter a recovery code in its place. # Manage recovery codes The Security page shows how many recovery codes you have left. Click Regenerate recovery codes to get a fresh set. You confirm with a current code, and the old set stops working immediately. # Turn off two-factor authentication Click Disable 2FA on the Security page and confirm with a current authenticator code or a recovery code. # Active sessions The Active sessions card lists every device signed in to your accou

title: Usage limits and monthly allowances
description: How Vidflow's monthly allowances work, what counts against each one, and what to do at a cap.
headings: What counts as an AI generation
What counts as a clip generation
body: Vidflow meters three things each month: episodes, AI generations, and clip generations. This page explains how the meters work, what counts against each one, and your options when you reach a cap. # How allowances work Allowances reset at the start of each calendar month (UTC). Episodes are metered per brand space. Your account's monthly total is your plan's per-brand allowance multiplied by the brand spaces on your subscription. AI generations and clip generations are account-wide pools. All of your brand spaces draw from the same two pools. Your plan sets the numbers: Plan Episodes per month per brand space AI generations per month Clip generations per month Free 1 20 3 Starter 2 150 30 Pro 8 600 120 Agency 8 2,500 500 See Plans and features for the full plan matrix, including seats and connected account slots. # What counts as an episode Every long-form source you add counts as one episode, whichever way it arrives: a direct upload, a YouTube import, or an auto-import from Google Drive or Dropbox. # What counts as an AI generation Each AI action draws from the same pool: AI writing: captions, text posts, ideas, scripts, and articles. AI image generation. Transcription of an uploaded file. Batch actions count once per item generated. Generating ten posts in one run uses ten AI generations, not one. # What counts as a clip generation Every clip you generate counts as one against the pool. # Connected account slots Your plan also caps connected social accounts per brand space

title: Auto-import from Google Drive or Dropbox
description: Watch a cloud folder and turn every new file into a tagged, captioned clip in your Content Library.
body: Point Vidflow at a folder in Google Drive or Dropbox and every new file becomes a clip in your Content Library , transcribed, AI-titled, and captioned in your brand voice, with your tags already applied. # How it works Each source watches one cloud folder. New files are imported hourly, or on demand, as clips in the Content Library . Each imported clip is transcribed, AI-titled, and captioned. Your brand voice is always included in the AI instructions. Tags you choose are applied automatically, so tag-fed schedules pick the content up on their own. See Schedules and queues . Files are never deleted from the source folder, and files already imported are skipped on later polls. By default a source only imports files added after you create it. Use Import existing to backfill older files. # Create a source In the navigation rail, open Content Library , then Auto-Import . Click New Source . Choose Google Drive or Dropbox and connect your account. Vidflow asks for read-only access. Pick the folder to watch in the Select Folder to Watch dialog. New files in that folder now import automatically every hour. # Source settings Open a source's settings to control what happens to each imported clip: Auto-Apply Tags : tags added to every imported clip. Create tags on the Tags page first if you have none. See Tags . Require Approval : imported clips are marked "Needs Approval" and will not publish until manually approved. See Approval status in your library . Platform-Specific Captions : pi

title: Bulk upload and CSV import
description: Add many files at once with the Bulk Upload wizard, or queue weeks of pre-written posts from a CSV.
body: The Bulk Upload page does two jobs. A drag-and-drop wizard turns a batch of files into library items with AI titles and captions, and a CSV importer creates posts you have already written. # The Bulk Upload wizard Note Note: The wizard is included on the Pro and Agency plans. The CSV importer further down the page works on any plan. See Plans and features . The wizard runs in three steps: Drop Files , Review , and Process . Click Make Content , then Bulk Upload . Drag your files in, or click to browse. Video, audio, images, plain text, markdown, and CSV files are supported. Set Link to source episode (optional but recommended) . Linking to an episode gives the AI context to write better titles and captions. If you stage clips without one, Vidflow shows a Missing Episode Context warning before it processes them. Click Review Files , check the list, then click Process All . Vidflow uploads the files, writes an AI title, caption, and tags for each one, and adds everything to your Content Library . When it finishes, use View in Library to see the results or Upload More to run another batch. # Import posts from a CSV The Import from spreadsheet (CSV) card on the same page works on any plan. Use it to queue weeks of posts at once. Click Template to download vidflow-import-template.csv and fill it in. Click Import CSV and drop your file in. Check the preview, then click Import . The button shows how many posts are ready. CSV columns Column What it does title Optional title for the p

title: Episodes vs. library content
description: How long-form source episodes relate to the posts, clips, and images you publish.
headings: Link content to its source episode
body: Vidflow separates what you make content from and what you publish. Episodes are your long-form sources. Library content is the individual pieces that go out to your channels. # Episodes: your source material An episode is a long-form source: a video or audio file you upload, or a video imported from YouTube. The episode carries the transcript, show notes, and metadata that the AI works from when it writes clips, captions, and posts. You create episodes by: Uploading a file. See Upload a video or audio file . Importing from YouTube. See Import from YouTube . Handing the Clip Generator a file or YouTube link. It saves the video to your library as a new episode, then clips it. See Generate clips from a long video . Each episode counts toward your plan's monthly episode allowance. See Usage limits and monthly allowances . # Library content: what you publish The Content Library holds the pieces you schedule and post: text posts, clips, images, carousels, and articles. These come from the Clip Generator, Magic Text Posts and the other Magic Tools, bulk upload, CSV import, auto-import sources, or manual creation with Create Content . # Where each shows up Episodes and content items live side by side in the Content Library , with views that filter to episodes or clips when you want one kind at a time. Opening an episode gives you four tabs: Details , Show Notes , Transcript , and Generated Clips . # Link content to its source episode Content that knows its source episode gets better

title: Import from YouTube
description: Pull videos from your connected YouTube channel into Vidflow as episodes with metadata and transcripts.
body: Bring videos from your own YouTube channel into Vidflow as episodes. Each import pulls the video's metadata and transcript only. The video file itself is never downloaded. # Before you start Connect your YouTube channel in Brand Settings > Connected Accounts . If YouTube is not connected, the import dialog asks you to connect it first. See Connect YouTube . YouTube import is a paid feature. See Plans and features . # Import videos On the Episodes page, click Import Episodes . You can also click Make Content , then Import Episodes , from anywhere in the app. Choose the Videos or Shorts tab. The dialog lists your channel's uploads. Click Load more to page further back. Select the videos you want. You can import up to 50 videos at a time. Videos already in your workspace show an Imported badge and cannot be selected again. Click Import into Library . Vidflow creates one episode per video and pulls transcripts in the background. You can keep working while they process. # What gets imported The video's metadata and its transcript. Not the video file. The episode links back to the original video on YouTube. Because there is no video file, the Native clip engine cannot clip a YouTube-imported episode. Upload the file if you plan to generate clips from it. See The Native clip engine . Note Note: Imported episodes count toward your plan's monthly episode allowance. If an import would go past it, the extra videos are skipped and Vidflow tells you how many. See Usage limits and monthly

title: Transcripts
description: How Vidflow transcribes your episodes, and how to retry, re-pull, or download a transcript.
body: Every episode gets a transcript, and much of Vidflow works from it: clips, captions, and AI writing all read the transcript. Transcription starts automatically after an upload or import. # How transcripts are generated Uploaded files are transcribed with AI speech-to-text. Episodes imported from YouTube pull the video's transcript instead. Vidflow transcribes in English today. Note Note: Transcribing an upload counts as one AI generation against your plan's monthly allowance. See Usage limits and monthly allowances . # Track transcription status A transcript moves through pending, processing, and completed states, or failed if something went wrong. The episode shows a status badge. While a transcription runs, the notifications bell in the header lists it as a Transcription job and links to your episodes. # Start or retry transcription If an episode shows "Transcript not yet generated", open it and click Transcribe Now . After a failed attempt, the same button retries. It is disabled while a transcription is already running. # Re-pull or download a transcript Open the episode from your Content Library and go to the Transcript tab: Pull transcript fetches a transcript for an episode that has none yet. Re-pull replaces the existing transcript. It pulls fresh YouTube captions or re-runs speech-to-text on the media. Download .txt saves the transcript as a plain text file. # What transcripts power Clip generation reads the transcript to find the strongest moments. See Generate clip

title: Upload a video or audio file
description: Add a long-form video or audio file to Vidflow and let transcription and AI metadata run automatically.
headings: Where you can start an upload
Start from a YouTube URL instead
body: Upload a long-form video or audio file and Vidflow saves it as an episode, starts the transcript, and drafts a title, description, and tags in the background. # Where you can start an upload Click Make Content at the top of the app, then Upload Episode . Click Upload Episode on the Episodes page. If the Episodes view of your Content Library is empty, use its Upload Video button. In the Clip Generator, the Upload file option uploads your file to the library as a new episode, then clips it. See Generate clips from a long video . # Upload a file Click Make Content , then Upload Episode . Drag your file into the dialog, or click Browse files . Vidflow accepts video and audio files: MP4, MOV, MP3, WAV, and M4A. Keep the tab open while the upload runs. Large files upload in chunks automatically, and the dialog shows progress as each part completes. Note Note: Uploads are capped at 1GB per file. When the upload finishes, Vidflow saves the episode as a draft immediately. Transcription and AI metadata generation (title, description, tags) continue in the background, so you can close the dialog and keep working. While the upload runs, you can also attach a clipping file with Add a clipping file (optional) . This is a clean version of the video without graphics, used later for AI clip generation. Each upload counts toward your plan's monthly episode allowance. See Usage limits and monthly allowances . # Start from a YouTube URL instead The same dialog accepts a YouTube URL. Paste the li

title: AI images and thumbnails
description: Generate images for posts in Image Studio or standalone in the Content Library, enhance clip thumbnails, build carousels, and make light image edits.
headings: Image Presets in Brand Settings (Pro and Agency)
body: Vidflow generates images where you already work: an Image Studio inside the post editor, a standalone generator in the Content Library, AI thumbnails in the clip editor, and a light image editor for crops and filters. Every image generation counts toward your plan's monthly AI allowance. See Usage limits and monthly allowances . # Image Studio Image Studio generates and refines images for a text or image post. Open a text or image post from your Content Library in the edit view. Click Image Studio . The studio opens beside the post; Close Studio hides it again. Describe the image in the prompt box, or click Magic prompt to draft a prompt from the post's title and caption. Pick an Aspect Ratio : 3:4, 4:5, 1:1, 9:16, 16:9, or 1.91:1. Each option notes the placements it suits, such as feed, story, or link preview. Optional: pick a Social style preset (curated art direction), or a Style template from your brand's saved Image Presets. With a template, the template controls the look and your prompt supplies the subject. Click Generate image . Every result lands in a strip of generations beside the preview, so you can compare attempts and return to an earlier one. From there: With an image selected, the main button becomes Refine this image : describe the change and Vidflow edits that image. Regenerate from scratch starts over from the prompt alone. Upload image adds your own file to the strip, so you can refine an existing graphic or use it as-is. Hover a thumbnail in the strip to

title: Blog Post Generator
description: Generate long-form articles from an episode, script, or prompt, then edit, copy, download, or save them to your library.
body: The Blog Post Generator turns an episode, script, or prompt into a long-form HTML article styled for LinkedIn Articles, Substack, or general publishing. You edit the draft directly in the preview, then copy it, download it, or save it to your Content Library. Find it as Blog Post under Magic Tools in the left rail. Note: Article generation is included on the Pro and Agency plans. On other plans the page opens but Generate Article is locked. See Plans and features . # Generate an article Pick a source: Episode , Script , or Prompt . Pick a platform style: LinkedIn Article (professional, high-signal, comment-friendly), Substack (newsletter-style, personal, opinionated), or General (the default). Pick a tone: Professional , Conversational (the default), Thought Leadership , or Educational . Pick a length: Short (around 600 words), Medium (around 1,200, the default), or Long (around 2,000). Add anything specific under Additional Instructions . Click Generate Article . With an episode as the source, the article is written from its transcript. The page confirms when a transcript is found and ready, and warns when the episode may not have one yet. See Transcripts . Note: Articles are written in your brand voice once it is set up; until then the page shows a banner and the AI writes in a generic voice. See Set up your brand voice . Each generation counts toward your plan's monthly AI allowance. See Usage limits and monthly allowances . # Edit the draft The preview is directly editabl

title: Idea Generator and Idea Library
description: Generate content ideas in your brand voice, save the keepers, and turn the best ones into scripts.
body: The Idea Generator brainstorms content ideas in your brand voice. The Idea Library keeps the ones you save, tracks which you have used, and feeds them straight into the Script Generator. Both live under Magic Tools in the left rail: Idea Generator , with Idea Library beneath it. # Generate ideas Open Idea Generator . Pick a Number of ideas : 3, 6, or 10 (6 is the default). Add a Topic or theme (optional) , or leave it blank to generate ideas based on your brand voice and past content. Click Generate Ideas . Two ways to steer the batch: Watch trending suggests angles to explore. Suggestions appear as pills; click one to add it to your topic. If nothing comes back, run it again. Start from a top performer appears once your analytics show top episodes. Pick an episode to riff on and the generator seeds itself from that episode's transcript. Each generated idea appears as a card. Click Save to keep it in your Idea Library, or Outline to expand a quick outline before you decide. Note: If your brand voice is not set up, the page shows a banner and the AI writes in a generic voice. See Set up your brand voice . Each generation also counts toward your plan's monthly AI allowance. See Usage limits and monthly allowances . # Work the library Open Idea Library . Ideas are split into Unused and Used tabs; an idea moves to Used once you turn it into a script. For each saved idea you can: Script : open the Script Generator seeded with this idea. Expand : have the AI develop the idea furthe

title: Magic Text Posts
description: Turn an episode, script, or prompt into a batch of text posts in your brand voice, saved to your library, a queue, or the calendar.
body: Magic Text Posts turns one source, an episode transcript, a saved script, or a plain prompt, into a batch of text posts written in your brand voice. You review the batch, then send everything to your Content Library, a queue, or the calendar in one step. Find it under Magic Tools in the left rail. # Before you start Set up your brand voice. Until you do, the page shows a banner reading "Your brand voice isn't set up yet" and the AI writes in a generic voice. See Set up your brand voice . To generate from an episode, that episode needs a transcript. Episodes that have one show a green transcript badge in the source picker. See Transcripts . Each generation counts toward your plan's monthly AI allowance. See Usage limits and monthly allowances . # Generate a batch Open Magic Text Posts and pick a content source: From Episode / Transcript , From Script , or Free Prompt / Topic . Leave Platform-agnostic on to get posts written to work on any platform, with no platform-specific hooks or formatting. Turning it off enables per-platform writing on the Pro and Agency plans (details below). Set a quantity for each post type you want. The Total Posts counter tracks the batch size. Under After Generating, Add Posts To... , pick a destination: Content Library , Queue , or Schedule . Click Generate Posts . Post types Quote : a direct quote or paraphrase from the source. Insight : a key lesson extracted and reframed. Tip : specific, actionable advice. Story : a short narrative moment. Quest

title: Script Generator
description: Draft full video and podcast scripts in your voice, refine any passage with AI, and save them to your script library.
body: The Script Generator writes a complete script for a YouTube video, podcast episode, or short-form video in your brand voice, sized to the runtime you want. You edit it as one document, refine any passage with AI, and save it to your script library. Find it under Magic Tools in the left rail. Script Library , beneath it, lists everything you have saved. # Generate a script Open Script Generator and click New Script . In Generation settings , pick a format: YouTube Video , Podcast Episode , or Short-Form . Set Target Video Length in minutes, from 1 to 180 (10 is the default). The panel estimates the spoken word count at the format's speaking pace: 145 words per minute for YouTube, 150 for podcast, 160 for short-form. Describe the topic in What's this script about? Click Generate Script . Note: Scripts read like you only after your brand voice is set up; until then the page shows a banner and the AI writes in a generic voice. See Set up your brand voice . Each generation counts toward your plan's monthly AI allowance. See Usage limits and monthly allowances . # Edit the script The script opens in a single-document editor with a live word count and an estimated runtime. Select any passage and right-click to Rewrite , Expand , or Shorten it. The updated text replaces your selection in place. Pull in saved ideas from the Idea Library panel on the right: drag an idea into the script, or click it to insert it. Regenerate Script rewrites the whole script from your current settings. #

title: Analytics overview
description: What the Analytics page tracks, when data appears, and what each card shows.
body: The Analytics page tracks your content output and audience growth for the current workspace. This article covers when data appears and what each card measures. # When data appears Analytics fill in as you connect social accounts and publish posts. A new workspace shows a "We're still collecting your data" card with a Connect accounts button until there is something to chart. Two things feed the page: Publishing through Vidflow. Post now, scheduled posts, and posts published by flows all count. Posts you publish directly on a platform, outside Vidflow, do not appear in the publishing cards. Connected accounts. Vidflow records follower counts for your connected accounts daily, which powers the Audience card. # Pick a period The period selector offers Last 7 days , Last 30 days , and Last 90 days . It defaults to 30. The period scopes the Audience change figures, the Posts card, and the Publishing Activity chart. The Total Episodes , Total Clips , and Published Posts cards are workspace totals and do not change with the period. # What each card shows Audience : current follower count per connected platform, with the change over the selected period. Total Episodes : every episode in the workspace. Total Clips : every clip generated in the workspace. Published Posts : everything published from this workspace. Posts : posts published inside the selected period. The card label names the window, for example "Posts (last 30d)". Publishing Activity : a bar chart of posts per day across

title: Branded client reports
description: Generate a performance report carrying your logo and brand color, then save it as a PDF from the print dialog.
body: Generate a client-ready performance report styled with your own branding, then save it as a PDF. The report is built from the same data as the Analytics page. # What the report includes The report carries your workspace logo (or your workspace initial if no logo is set), your workspace name, and your brand color. The header reads "Performance Report" with the covered period and the date it was generated. Sections: Overview : posts published, scheduled, awaiting approval, episodes, clips created, and total content. Audience : followers and change per platform. Published by platform : how published posts split across platforms. Top episodes : your top episodes ranked by clips generated. Content pipeline : draft, scheduled, and published counts. The footer shows your workspace name and a small "Prepared with Vidflow" line. Set your logo and brand color in your workspace's brand settings; the same branding covers the client approval portal. See Set up your brand voice . # Generate the report Open Analytics from the app rail. Pick the period the report should cover: Last 7 days , Last 30 days , or Last 90 days . Click Branded report . The report opens in a new browser tab, and the print dialog appears automatically. In the print dialog, choose your browser's save-as-PDF destination and save. Important Important: The report opens as a pop-up. If your browser blocks it, Vidflow shows "Allow pop-ups to open the report." Allow pop-ups for app.vidflow.io and click Branded report again.

title: Approval status in your library
description: How approval badges work across the Content Library and what they mean for scheduled publishing.
body: Every content item can carry an approval state, and that state decides whether scheduled publishing will touch it. This article covers the badges, how content enters review, and how approval interacts with publishing. # The badges Needs Approval : the item is waiting for review. Inside the item view the badge reads Pending Approval . Approved : the item has been cleared and publishes normally. Rejected : the item has been marked as not cleared. Rejected items are held back from scheduled publishing the same way pending items are. You see these badges in the item view in your Content Library and on the client review page. In the library toolbar, the approval filter switches between All Approval States , Needs Approval , and Approved , and the sort menu includes Unapproved First . Approval state changes when your client acts on the review page: approving an item marks it Approved , and undoing that returns it to Needs Approval . # Send an item into review Open the item in your Content Library . Click Submit for Approval in the footer. The button appears while the item has no approval state yet. The item is marked Pending Approval . To put the item in front of a client, add it to an approval link. See Create and share an approval link . Content can also enter review automatically: Auto-import sources have a require-approval option, so imported files arrive as Needs Approval instead of publishing unseen. See Auto-import from Google Drive or Dropbox . In Flows, the Approvals node

title: Client approvals overview
description: Send your client one link to review and approve content before it publishes. No client account needed.
body: Approvals let you put finished content in front of a client from a single shareable link. The client reviews on any device, with no Vidflow account and no login. # Why approval links If you run content for clients, the review step is usually the slowest part of the pipeline: screenshots pasted into email threads, "any word on last week's posts?" follow-ups, and change requests scattered across three channels. An approval link replaces that. You pick the content, Vidflow generates one URL, and everything the client needs lives behind it. The page carries your logo, your workspace name, and your brand color. Vidflow's branding does not appear, so to your client it reads as your review portal. # How it works Create a link from the Approvals page. Give it a title, pick the content, and set an optional expiry between 1 and 90 days. Send the URL to your client through whatever channel you already use. The client opens it in a browser and approves items or leaves comments. There is no reject button; change requests come back as comments. Every approval and comment saves the moment it happens. There is no submit step for the client to forget. You check progress from the Approvals page. Vidflow does not send you a notification when a client approves or comments, so build the check into your routine. Once an item is approved, it is cleared to publish. Scheduled posts that are still waiting on approval are held back, then go out after approval. # Where approvals plug in Schedules : ever

title: What your client sees
description: The review page from your client's side, including approving, undoing, and commenting, and how you see the results.
body: When your client opens an approval link, they land on a review page branded as yours. This article walks through that page and what happens with everything they do on it. # No login, your brand The link opens in any browser. Your client does not create an account, set a password, or sign in to anything. The page header shows your workspace logo, your workspace name, and the link title. If you have not uploaded a logo, a check mark in your brand color stands in. The progress bar picks up your brand color too. Vidflow's branding does not appear; to your client, this is your review portal. Set your logo and brand color in your workspace's brand settings. See Set up your brand voice . # What your client can do Each content item appears as a card with its media (video player or image), its caption, a status badge, and a comments panel. Approve : the green Approve button marks the item approved. The badge updates immediately. Undo an approval : an approved item's button turns into a green confirmation. Clicking it again returns the item to Needs Approval . Approve All : while items are still open, an "Approve all remaining items" row at the top offers Approve All . Comment : type into the comment box and click Add comment . Comments stay attached to the item, and you see them when you open the link. There is no reject button on the review page. A client who wants changes leaves a comment on the item and holds off on approving it. Change requests arrive as comments. Every action sav

title: Create and share an approval link
description: Bundle content into a review link, set an optional expiry, and manage the link after you send it.
body: Create a link that bundles content for a client to review, then copy it and send it. This article covers creating, sharing, closing, and reopening links. # Before you start Approvals are available on paid plans, Starter and up. On the Free plan the Create Approval Link button is locked and prompts you to upgrade. See Plans and features . The content you want reviewed needs to be in your Content Library first. # Create the link In the app rail, open Approvals (in the Publish section). Click Create Approval Link . If this is your first link, the page shows a Create First Link button instead. Enter a Link Title , for example "Week 12 Content Review". The client sees this title when they open the link. Optionally set Expires In (days) to a number from 1 to 90. Leave it blank and the link never expires. Under Select Content , open the picker and select the items the client should review. Click Create Link . # Share it The dialog switches to Approval Link Created and shows the URL in a Shareable Link field. Click Copy to put the URL on your clipboard. Click Preview Link to open the review page yourself and check it before sending. Send the URL to your client by email, text, or any other channel. It is a plain URL; nothing else is required. Click Done to close the dialog. Important Important: Anyone who has the URL can open it, approve, and comment. There is no login gate. Share it only with the people who should sign off. # Manage existing links Every link you have created appears

title: Clips troubleshooting
description: Fix the most common problems with generating and editing clips.
headings: "OpusClip isn't connected for this workspace yet"
"Native clips need the actual video file"
You have reached your monthly clip allowance
body: Common problems when generating or editing clips, and what to do about each one. # "OpusClip isn't connected for this workspace yet" This appears in the Clip Generator when you select the OpusClip engine but no OpusClip API key is connected for the current workspace. The Generate Clips button is disabled until you fix this. Fix it one of two ways: Go to Workspace Settings > Connections and connect this workspace's OpusClip API key. See Using OpusClip with Vidflow . Or switch to the Native engine, which needs no connector. # "Native clips need the actual video file" This appears when you try to run the Native engine on an episode that was added as a link (for example, a YouTube URL) with no uploaded media. Native cannot work from a link alone. Fix it one of two ways: Upload the video file to that episode, then run Native again. Or switch to the OpusClip engine, which can fetch link-only sources itself. # Generation seems stuck After you click Generate Clips , the status card reads "Generating your clips." with "This usually takes a few minutes." Most runs finish within that window. Note Note: If clips have not appeared in your Content Library after roughly 30 minutes, re-run the generation, or contact support if it happens again. Do not assume a failed run will always show an error message on screen. Before re-running, check the Clips tab in your Content Library directly. Clips are added there automatically as soon as they finish rendering, and this is more reliable than watch

title: Edit clips: captions, reframing, thumbnails
description: Open a generated clip to rewrite its captions, adjust reframing, swap the video, or pick a new thumbnail.
body: Once a clip lands in your Content Library, open it to rewrite the title and captions in your brand voice, adjust how it is reframed and captioned, pick a new thumbnail, or swap the underlying video. # Open a clip From the Content Library, open a clip item to reach the clip detail dialog. It has three tabs: Details , Reframe , and Captions . Details : review the clip, approve it, share it, and render edited exports. Reframe : adjust the crop used to reframe the clip. Captions : edit caption timing. # AI Clip Writing The AI Clip Writing panel generates titles and captions from the clip's transcript, using your workspace's voice profile. Choose a Caption Style . Click Generate Title for a title, or generate captions per platform, or click Generate All Platform Captions to do every connected platform at once. The panel shows badges for the active style ("Style: {style}") and confirms "Voice profile active" when your brand voice is applied. # Render your edits From the Details tab, under Rendered Export : Make your reframing and caption edits in the other tabs. Optionally check Add on-screen hook text during render and enter the text to burn in. Click Render Edited Clip . This burns your caption and reframing edits into a fresh MP4. Once rendering finishes, click Open Rendered MP4 to view it. # Thumbnails From the clip's media tools, you can: Use this frame as thumbnail : capture the current video frame as the clip's thumbnail. AI thumbnail for Shorts : generate a branded, high-CT

title: Generate clips from a long video
description: Turn an episode, an uploaded file, or a YouTube link into short clips with the Clip Generator.
body: Use the Clip Generator to turn a long-form video into a batch of short clips. Pick a source, choose an engine, set your options, and Vidflow finds the moments and renders the clips for you. # Before you start You need a source video: an existing episode with a video file, an uploaded video or audio file, or a YouTube link. You also need at least one clip engine available. Vidflow ships two: Native , Vidflow's in-house engine. No setup, works out of the box. OpusClip , a third-party engine that requires your own OpusClip API key connected for the workspace. See The Native clip engine and Using OpusClip with Vidflow for what each one offers. # Open the Clip Generator From the app rail, click Clip Generator . Or, from an episode's detail page, click Generate Clips to open the Clip Generator with that episode preselected. Or, from a Content Library card's menu, choose Generate Clips . # Pick a source Under Source , choose one of three tabs: Existing episode : select any episode in your library that already has a video file. Upload file : click Choose video or audio file to upload a new file. This adds the file to your library as a new episode, then clips it. YouTube link : paste a YouTube URL and click Add . This adds the video to your library as a new episode, then clips it. Note Note: A YouTube link alone does not give you a video file to work with. If you plan to use the Native engine, upload the file directly, or import it as an episode with the video attached instead of a ba

title: The Native clip engine
description: Generate clips with Vidflow's built-in engine, no third-party account required.
body: Native is Vidflow's in-house clip engine. It selects moments from the transcript, reframes the video, burns in captions, and renders the clips on Vidflow's own infrastructure. There is no third-party key to connect. Beta note Beta note: The Native engine is in beta. It is the default engine in the Clip Generator. # What you need Native needs the actual video file uploaded to the episode. It does not work from a link-only source. Important Important: If your episode was added as a YouTube link with no uploaded media, Native shows this message: "Native clips need the actual video file. This episode is a link (for example YouTube) with no uploaded media." Upload the video file for this episode, or switch to the OpusClip engine, which can fetch link-only sources itself. # Options From the Clip Generator, select the Native tab, then set: Number of clips : a number from 1 to 20. Default is 8. Clip length : Any length (default), Under 1 min , 1 to 3 min , or 3 to 5 min . Caption style : Standard (house default) , Box Background , Bold Center Pop , Karaoke Band , Clean Minimal , Hype Impact , or Editorial Serif . Click Generate Clips to start the run. # How it works Native runs in two stages. First, a selection pass reads the transcript and picks brand-aware moments. Then each selected moment goes through an automatic render job: reframing to vertical, captions burned in using your chosen style, and a thumbnail pulled from about a third of the way into the clip. Finished clips appear

title: Using OpusClip with Vidflow
description: Connect your own OpusClip API key to generate clips with brand templates, ClipAnything, and more.
body: OpusClip is a third-party clip engine you connect to Vidflow with your own API key. Because it fetches source video itself, it works for YouTube-link episodes as well as uploaded files. Each workspace connects its own OpusClip account. Your clips, credits, and brand templates stay on your OpusClip account, not a shared Vidflow key. # Connect your OpusClip API key Go to Workspace Settings , then the Connections tab. Find the OpusClip card. If it is not connected, you will see Connect required . Click Connect OpusClip account . Paste your OpusClip API key. You can find it in the OpusClip dashboard under Settings > API . Optionally add an Organization ID (the x-opus-org-id header), needed only for multi-org OpusClip accounts. Click Test & Save . Vidflow validates the key with a zero-credit call before saving it. Once connected, the card shows a Connected badge and a masked version of your key. Click Replace key to swap it, or Disconnect to remove it. Note Note: OpusClip requires an OpusClip Pro (Beta) or Business plan with API access on your OpusClip account. Credits are billed by OpusClip directly and are metered separately from your Vidflow plan. If OpusClip is not connected when you try to generate, the Clip Generator disables the Generate Clips button and shows: "OpusClip isn't connected for this workspace yet. Add this workspace's OpusClip API key in Workspace Settings, Connections. Or switch to the Native engine above, no connector needed." # Options From the Clip Generato

title: Let clients connect their own accounts
description: Mint a shareable link so a client or talent can connect their own social accounts to your workspace without signing in to Vidflow.
body: A Vidflow Connect link lets someone who owns the social accounts, a client, a talent, a partner brand, connect their own platforms to your workspace without ever signing in to Vidflow itself. You send the link, they sign in with each platform on their end, and the accounts show up ready to publish in your workspace. Note Note: Connect links are included on the Pro and Agency plans. See Plans and features . # Create a link Open Brand Settings . Click the Accounts tab. Click the Vidflow Connect sub-tab. Type a label for the link, for example the client or brand name. Click New Connect Link . Copy the link from the table, or click the preview icon to open it in a new tab. By default a connect link never expires. Set an expiry in days when you create it if you want the link to stop working after a set window. # What the recipient sees When someone opens the link, they land on a page titled Connect Your Social Media Accounts , with a short instruction to sign in with each platform so posts can publish automatically. Two ways to add accounts are offered: Connect with sign-in (recommended): real OAuth buttons for YouTube, Instagram & Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, and X (Twitter). These go through each platform's actual login screen, the same as connecting from inside Vidflow, so the resulting account can publish immediately. Any platform your workspace has not set up shows as Not set up yet and cannot be clicked. Or add manually : label-only entries for anyone who cannot sign in right

title: Connect Instagram and Facebook
description: Link Instagram and Facebook together through one Meta connection and see what each platform can publish.
body: Instagram and Facebook connect through a single Meta sign-in. One connection can link both platforms at once, since Vidflow discovers your Facebook Page and any Instagram professional account attached to it during the same flow. # What you need before connecting A Facebook Page . Vidflow publishes to Facebook through a Page, not a personal profile. For Instagram, a professional (business) Instagram account linked to that Facebook Page . A personal Instagram account with no Page connection will not appear. If your Instagram is not yet set up as a professional account linked to a Page, do that in Meta's own settings first, then connect in Vidflow. # Connect steps Open Workspace Settings . Click the Connections tab. Click Facebook & Instagram . Sign in on Meta's page and approve access, including access to your Pages. You are returned to Vidflow. Vidflow links your Facebook Page automatically, and links Instagram too if a professional Instagram account is attached to that Page. # What Vidflow can publish Instagram : video posts published as Reels, with a caption. Facebook : video posts to your Page, with a title and description. Both need a video file. Vidflow does not publish image-only or text-only posts to either platform today. # Limits enforced Instagram caption: 2200 characters. Facebook title: 255 characters. Facebook description: 5000 characters. A video file is required for both platforms. # Token lifetime and reconnect Meta issues a long-lived token when you connect, a

title: Connect LinkedIn
description: Link LinkedIn to Vidflow to publish posts, and know when to expect a reconnect.
body: Connect LinkedIn to publish posts to your LinkedIn profile from Vidflow. # Connect steps Open Workspace Settings . Click the Connections tab. Click LinkedIn . Sign in on LinkedIn's page and approve access. You are returned to Vidflow with LinkedIn listed as connected. # What Vidflow can publish Text and video posts to your connected LinkedIn profile, from Post now on library content and from scheduled dispatch on both content items and legacy queue posts. # Limits enforced Title: 200 characters. # Token lifetime and reconnect LinkedIn tokens last roughly 60 days. Expect a periodic reconnect prompt as that window closes. When LinkedIn expires the authorization, Vidflow pauses publishing to that account and emails you explaining what happened. Reconnecting from the Connections tab picks back up anything waiting on your schedule. # Troubleshooting LinkedIn posting stopped working after a couple months. This is the expected reconnect cycle. Reconnect from the Connections tab, no data is lost. I reconnected but posts are still failing. Confirm the reconnect completed and LinkedIn shows as connected rather than pending. If it still fails, contact support with the timestamp of the failed post. # Related articles Connect your social accounts Post now Post statuses and failed posts Fix publishing problems

title: Connect your social accounts
description: Where to connect social accounts in Vidflow, what statuses mean, and how reconnecting and disconnecting work.
body: Connect your social accounts once per workspace so Vidflow can publish and schedule content on your behalf. This article covers where connections live and what happens as accounts age, expire, or get disconnected. For steps on a specific platform, use the per-platform articles linked below. # Where to connect Open Workspace Settings . Click the Connections tab. Choose a platform to connect: YouTube, Instagram & Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, or X (Twitter). Clicking a platform starts that platform's real sign-in flow. You will leave Vidflow briefly, approve access on the platform's own page, and land back in Vidflow once the connection completes. Every workspace is its own brand. Accounts you connect in one workspace do not appear in another. # Two kinds of accounts Vidflow distinguishes accounts that can actually publish from accounts that are only a label: Connected accounts finished the real sign-in flow. These can post and schedule content. Manually added accounts (added by typing a name, or submitted through a client connect link without signing in) are placeholders. They cannot publish until someone completes the sign-in flow for that platform. If you see a platform pill grayed out when posting, check whether that account was ever actually connected with sign-in. # Account slots per plan Each plan allows a limited number of connected social accounts. If you try to connect one more than your plan allows, you will see: Important Important: "Your plan allows {limit} connected

title: Connect TikTok
description: Link TikTok to Vidflow and understand the settings every TikTok post requires.
body: Connect TikTok to publish and schedule videos to your TikTok account directly from Vidflow. Beta note Beta note: TikTok posts currently publish as private (visible only to you) while the TikTok app review completes. Vidflow marks the post and emails you once a post is affected, and switches to normal publishing automatically when the review finishes. # Connect steps Open Workspace Settings . Click the Connections tab. Click TikTok . Sign in on TikTok's page and approve access. You are returned to Vidflow with TikTok listed as connected. # What Vidflow can publish Vidflow publishes video posts to TikTok, both from Post now on library content and from scheduled dispatch. A video file is required, TikTok cannot post text-only or image-only content. # The TikTok settings panel Every TikTok post, whether posted now or scheduled, includes a required settings panel: Posting as {your TikTok name} , confirming which account will post. Who can view this post : the exact choices come from your TikTok account's own settings and typically include Public, Friends, Followers, and Only you. Vidflow only offers the audiences your TikTok account allows. Allow comments toggle. Allow Duet and Allow Stitch toggles (video posts only). Disclose video content : turn this on if the post promotes a brand, product, or service, then choose Your brand (you are promoting yourself or your own business) or Branded content (a paid partnership or promoting another brand). Branded content can never be posted p

title: Connect X
description: Link X (Twitter) to Vidflow to publish posts and know the media size limit.
body: Connect X (Twitter) to publish text and media posts from Vidflow. # Connect steps Open Workspace Settings . Click the Connections tab. Click X (Twitter) . Sign in on X's page and approve access. X always shows its login screen here, so you can connect a different or additional account even if one is already signed in on your device. You are returned to Vidflow with X listed as connected. # What Vidflow can publish Text and media posts to your connected X account, from Post now on library content and from scheduled dispatch on both content items and legacy queue posts. # Limits enforced Post text: 280 characters. Scheduled content items are automatically sliced to this length. Media: 512MB hard cap. Video uploads process asynchronously on X's side, which can take up to about 2 minutes before the post finishes. # Token lifetime and reconnect X issues rotating refresh tokens, and Vidflow refreshes them automatically in the background, deduplicated so that overlapping refresh attempts cannot burn through the token family. Most connections need no attention. Note Note: Posting media requires the media.write permission scope. If your X account was connected before Vidflow requested that scope, media posts will not work until you reconnect. Text-only posts are unaffected. If X revokes the authorization outright, Vidflow pauses that account and emails you. Reconnecting resumes anything waiting on your schedule. # Troubleshooting Media posts fail but text posts work fine. Reconnect yo

title: Connect YouTube
description: Link your YouTube channel to Vidflow to publish episodes and schedule clips.
body: Connect YouTube to upload full episodes and publish scheduled clips to your channel from Vidflow. # What you need before connecting A Google account with a YouTube channel. You will sign in with that Google account and grant Vidflow permission to upload and read your channel. # Connect steps Open Workspace Settings . Click the Connections tab. Click YouTube . Sign in with the Google account that owns your channel and approve access. You are returned to Vidflow with YouTube listed as connected. # What Vidflow can publish YouTube works a little differently from the other platforms: Full episodes publish from the episode's own Publishing tab, not from Post now on library content. You choose the privacy for each upload: Public , Private , or Unlisted . Vidflow defaults new uploads to Private so you can review before making them public. Scheduled clips publish through your regular schedules and queues, and go out as Shorts-eligible videos set to Public. # Limits enforced Scheduled clip title: 100 characters. Scheduled clip description: 4900 characters. A video file is required. # Token lifetime and reconnect Vidflow refreshes your YouTube authorization automatically, about 5 minutes before it would otherwise expire, so most connections never need attention. If Google reports that the authorization itself was revoked, for example if you removed Vidflow's access in your Google account, Vidflow pauses that account and emails you. Reconnect from the Connections tab and anything waitin

title: Bulk actions
description: Re-tag, set default accounts, copy between workspaces, or delete many library items in one pass with Bulk Manage.
body: Bulk actions change many library items in one pass instead of opening them one at a time. They run from the Bulk Manage Content dialog. Note Note: Bulk actions are available on plans that include them. See Plans and features . # Open Bulk Manage In the rail, click Content Library , then Bulk Actions . The library opens with the Bulk Manage Content dialog and four actions: Re-Tag Content : add or override tags on the selected items. Select Default Accounts : add or override the default accounts the selected items post to. Delete : delete multiple items at once. Copy Content to Other Workspaces : copy the selected items into another workspace. # Select the content Every action starts with the same picker. Click Select content to manage , then check the items to include. Use the search box to narrow the list, and Select All or Deselect All to move faster. The dialog shows how many items are selected. # Re-tag content Choose Re-Tag Content and select your items. Pick the tags to apply. You can create a new tag inline. Check Remove Existing Content Tags to replace the items' tags instead of adding to them. Apply. A confirmation reports how many items were re-tagged. See Tags for how tags drive filters, schedules, and automation. # Set default accounts Choose Select Default Accounts and select your items. Pick the connected accounts the items should post to. Select All Accounts picks everything. Check Remove Existing Default Accounts to replace instead of add. Apply. # Copy content

title: Edit a post
description: Open any library item to change its title, caption, platforms, tags, and status, or send it for approval.
body: Every item in the Content Library opens in the same editor. This article walks through editing a text post or image, then summarizes how clips and episodes differ. # Open the editor Open the Content Library and click any item. It opens read-only first. Click Edit Content to make changes. Use the arrows at the top to step to the previous or next item. A counter shows your position in the current list. Click Save when you are done, or Cancel to discard changes. # What you can edit Internal Title : a name for your own reference. It is optional and never published. Caption (image posts) or Post Content (text posts): the text that publishes. A character counter tracks the limit for each platform. Tags : click tag pills to add or remove them. See Tags . Post to : toggle the platforms this item publishes to. Status : Draft , Queued , Scheduled , or Published . See Content Library basics for what each means. On image posts, Image Studio opens an image panel beside the editor. See AI images and thumbnails . Note Note: When TikTok is toggled on under Post to , a TikTok settings panel appears for audience, comments, and content disclosure. See Post now for the details. # Per-platform captions One item can carry a different caption for each platform. Turn on the Platform specific captions switch. A tab appears for each platform selected under Post to . Write a caption in each tab. Leave a tab blank to use the main post content. Each tab has its own character counter against that platform

title: Content Library basics
description: Find, filter, and sort everything in your Vidflow library, and understand what the status and approval labels mean.
body: The Content Library holds everything in a workspace: text posts, images, clips, articles, and the episodes they came from. This article shows you how to find things fast and what the status labels mean. Open it from the rail: click Content Library , then All Content . # Tabs Three tabs split the library by what you are looking at: All Content shows every item in the workspace. Episodes shows only your long-form source videos. Clips shows only clips generated from episodes. Episodes are sources, not posts. See Episodes vs. library content for how the two relate. If the library is empty, the page offers shortcuts such as Upload Video , Import from YouTube , and Magic Text Posts to create your first content. # Views and sorting The view button on the left of the filter row switches between Grid View , Compact View , and List View . The sort menu next to it orders items by Date Created , Title A-Z , Unused First , Unapproved First , or Media Type . # Filters The same sort menu also holds the filters. They stack with search: All Types , or one content type: Text , Articles , Images , Clips , Episodes . All Tags , or one of your tags. The Manage Tags button beside the menu creates and edits tags. See Tags . All Approval States , Needs Approval , or Approved . A separate From episode select narrows the library to content created from one source episode. Pick All sources to clear it. # Search The search box matches titles by default. The toggle beside it switches scope: Titles search

title: Tags
description: Create color-coded tags to organize your library and route content into schedules, imports, and Flows.
body: Tags organize your content library into custom categories: series, campaigns, content pillars, or anything else that fits your brand. They also do routing work, since schedules, auto-import, and Flows can all act on tags. Tags are available on every plan. # Create a tag Open the Content Library and click Manage Tags in the filter row. Under New Tag , type a name and pick a color. Ten preset swatches are offered, or click the color square for a custom color. Click Add . # Edit or delete a tag In the same Manage Tags dialog, the Existing Tags list shows every tag in the workspace. Use the controls on a tag's row to rename it, change its color, or delete it. Deleting asks for confirmation and removes the tag from all content that carries it. The content itself is not deleted. # Tag your content One item: open it and click the tag pills in the editor. See Edit a post . Many items: use Re-Tag Content in Bulk Manage. See Bulk actions . Episodes: the episode's Details tab has a Tags field. Separate tags with commas. Automatically: auto-import sources and Flows can tag content for you (see below). # Where tags work Library filters : narrow the library to one tag from the sort and filter menu. See Content Library basics . Schedules : a schedule can be fed by tags, so only content carrying any of those tags enters that queue. See Schedules and queues . Auto-import : a source can tag everything it brings in. See Auto-import from Google Drive or Dropbox . Flows : the Tag Content node app

title: Build your first flow
description: Create a flow in the editor, connect nodes and router branches, add branch filters, then save and run it.
body: Build a flow that turns each new episode into content automatically. This walkthrough covers the editor, connecting nodes, router branches, branch filters, and the first run. Note Note: Flows require the Pro or Agency plan. See Plans and features . # Create the flow Open Flows in the sidebar. Click New Flow , or click Use Template on a template to start from a prebuilt pipeline. In the left panel, enter a Workflow Name and an optional Description . In the toolbar, pick the trigger: Episode Upload , Google Drive File , or Manual Trigger . # Get to know the editor The left panel holds the node palette, grouped into Triggers, Processing, Publishing, Logic, and Reviews. The toolbar has the trigger picker, the Active switch, an Episode ID for Run field, undo and redo, the node legend, Snap , Tidy up , zoom controls with a recenter button, Run , and Save . A few useful controls: Drag the empty canvas to pan, and scroll to zoom. Snap aligns nodes to the grid while you drag. Tidy up auto-arranges the whole flow into clean columns. The question mark button opens the Node Type Legend , a searchable catalog of every node. Clicking a node there adds it to the canvas. Keyboard shortcuts: Cmd+S (Ctrl+S on Windows) saves, Cmd+Z undoes, Cmd+Y redoes, Delete removes the selected node or connection, and G toggles the grid. # Add and connect nodes Drag a node from the palette onto the canvas, or click it to place it. Drop the node near an existing node's output and it connects automatically. Dr

title: Flow nodes reference
description: Every node in the flow editor palette with its options, grouped the way the palette groups them.
body: Every node you can place in a flow, with its options. Nodes are listed in the same groups as the editor palette: Triggers, Processing, Publishing, Logic, and Reviews. Two things apply to every node: Each node has a Label field in its configuration panel so you can rename it on the canvas. Generated content travels down the flow as drafts. Enrichment nodes (captions, images, tags) modify those drafts, but nothing is stored until the drafts reach a Save or Post node. # Triggers A flow has one trigger, chosen in the toolbar. The trigger nodes in the palette match the same three options. Episode Upload Starts the flow whenever a new episode is created in this workspace. No configuration. The flow's Active switch must be on for it to fire, and each run waits for the episode's transcript before starting; see Runs, history, and troubleshooting . Google Drive File Starts the flow from a file dropped into a Google Drive folder. Folder ID : the Drive folder to watch. Beta note Beta note: The Google Drive trigger is in beta. Its folder watcher is not wired up yet, so these flows do not fire on their own. You can still build and save one, and run it manually. Manual Trigger Runs on demand. When you start the run you pick an episode (uses its transcript) or a saved script (uses the script text) as the source. No configuration. # Processing Generate Clips Generates short clips from the source episode. Pick the Engine first; the options change with it. Engine : OpusClip or Native (Beta) . W

title: Runs, history, and troubleshooting
description: How flow runs start, why episode-upload runs wait for the transcript, and how to read the run history.
body: Every execution of a flow is a run. This article covers how runs start, the waiting state on episode uploads, the history log, and the most common failures. # How runs start Automatically. An Episode Upload flow with its Active switch on queues a run whenever a new episode is created in the workspace. Manually from the Flows page. Click Run on the flow card, pick an episode (uses its transcript) or a saved script (uses the script text), then click Run flow . Manually from the editor. Enter an episode's ID in Episode ID for Run and click Run . Without an ID the run starts with no source, and steps that need one are skipped. A flow must be saved before it can run. In a brand-new flow, Run stays disabled until the first Save . # The waiting state Episode-upload runs do not start the moment the episode is created. The transcript is still being generated at that point, and the AI nodes need it. So the run is held in a Waiting state, shown as an amber badge on the flow card. Once the episode's transcript completes, Vidflow waits a few more minutes for metadata to finish populating, then starts the run. If the episode is not ready within about 30 minutes of upload, or transcription fails, the run is marked failed and you get a notification. Fix the transcript first (see Transcripts ), then run the flow manually. Manual runs skip the wait entirely and start right away, because you picked a source that already exists. # Run statuses A run moves through these statuses, visible on the f

title: Flows overview
description: What Flows automate, which plans include them, and how triggers, templates, and runs fit together.
body: Flows are Vidflow's automation builder. You draw a pipeline once, and every new episode comes out the other side as clips, text posts, and images, saved to your library and queued for publishing. # What a flow is A flow is a chain of nodes on a canvas. It starts with a trigger, runs your content through processing nodes (clips, text posts, blog posts, captions, images, tags), and ends in a publishing node that saves the results. Until a Save node runs, nothing generated by the flow is stored, so every flow should end in Save to Library , Save to Queue , or another publishing node. Flows belong to a workspace. Open Flows in the sidebar to see the flows for the workspace you are in. Note Note: Flows require the Pro or Agency plan. See Plans and features . The requirement is enforced when you create a flow and when you run one. # Triggers Episode Upload starts the flow whenever a new episode is created in the workspace. The flow's Active switch must be on. These runs wait for the episode transcript to finish before they start; see Runs, history, and troubleshooting . Manual Trigger runs on demand. When you click Run , you pick an episode or a saved script as the source. Google Drive File starts a flow from a file dropped in a Drive folder. Beta note Beta note: Episode-upload and manual triggers are fully live. The Google Drive trigger is in beta: its folder watcher is not wired up yet, so Drive flows do not fire on their own (you can still run them manually). Webhook and script

title: Getting access to Vidflow
description: Vidflow is invite-only during the beta. Here is how the waitlist and invite process works.
headings: If you try to sign in without an invite
body: Vidflow is currently invite-only, with new accounts onboarded in waves from the waitlist. # How access works today There is no open signup. Every sign-in is checked against an invite list, whether you sign in with Google or with email and password. If your email is not on that list, you land on the waitlist page instead of the app. Beta note Beta note: Vidflow is in an invite-only beta. Access opens in waves as capacity allows. # If you try to sign in without an invite If you attempt to sign in with an email that has not been invited, you are redirected to a waitlist page. Depending on your situation, you will see one of two messages: "Vidflow is invite only." if your email has never been seen before. "Your account has not been approved yet." if your email was already caught by the process once. Either way, the page lets you join the waitlist right there. Submitting the form always confirms your spot, whether or not your email was already known to Vidflow. # After you join the waitlist There is no published timeline for when waitlisted accounts get invited. Access is granted in waves, and the Vidflow team decides when to pull from the waitlist based on capacity. # When you get invited Once you are approved, you will receive an email with the subject line "Your Vidflow access is ready." The email confirms you have been granted access and tells you to sign in at app.vidflow.io using that same email address. After you receive that email Go to app.vidflow.io. Sign in using the ex

title: Finding your way around
description: A tour of the rail, the Make Content button, notifications, and where to get help inside Vidflow.
body: Once you are inside a workspace, everything in Vidflow is reachable from a left-hand navigation rail, a global Make Content button, and a notifications bell. # The navigation rail The rail has seven sections: Home : opens your Dashboard, with your upcoming schedule, quick actions, and recent activity. Content Library : All Content, Bulk Actions, and Auto-Import. Publish : Calendar and Schedules under Schedule, Approvals under Review. Magic Tools : Clip Generator, Idea Generator, Idea Library, Script Generator, and Script Library under Create; Magic Text Posts and Blog Post under Distribute. Flows : opens the Flows automation builder directly. Analytics : Overview and Top Content. Settings : opens Workspace Settings. Sections marked as panels expand to show their items; Flows opens straight to its own page. # The Make Content button The Make Content button in the global header is the fastest way to start something new, from anywhere in the app. It opens a menu with: Upload Episode , to upload a video or audio file as a new episode. Import Episodes , to import existing content, such as from YouTube. Upload Content , to add a finished piece of content directly to your library. Magic Text Posts , to jump straight to generating text posts. Create Image Post , to start a new image-based post. Bulk Upload , to add many pieces of content at once. # The notifications bell The bell in the header tracks jobs running in the background, most commonly transcription. When an episode is tran

title: Quickstart: one video to a week of content
description: The fastest path from your first sign-in to a week of scheduled content, one step at a time.
headings: What you have at the end
body: This is the golden path through Vidflow: from your first sign-in to a full week of content sitting on your calendar. Follow it once, in order, and every later visit is faster. Note Note: The dashboard also includes a short in-app setup guide that walks through steps 2 to 4 below with direct links to each screen. Use whichever you prefer, they cover the same ground. # The path Get invited and sign in. Vidflow is invite-only during the beta. Once you have access, sign in with Google or with an email and password. See Getting access to Vidflow and Signing in and resetting your password . Confirm your workspace. Your first sign-in lands you on the Brand Spaces screen, and your first workspace is included on every plan. If you are setting up for a single brand, you likely already have what you need; agencies create one workspace per client brand. See Workspaces and brand spaces . Set up your brand voice. Open Workspace Settings and fill in your Brand Profile: brand name, target audience, and your brand sheet. This is what keeps the AI from writing in a generic voice. See Set up your brand voice . Upload or import your first video. Use the Upload Episode option from the Make Content menu to upload a file, or import an existing YouTube video. Either way, this becomes your first episode. See Upload a video or audio file and Import from YouTube . Wait for the transcript. Vidflow transcribes your episode automatically after upload or import. Everything generated from this episode, clip

title: Signing in and resetting your password
description: How to sign in with Google or email and password, and how to reset a forgotten password.
headings: Sign in with email and password
body: Vidflow supports two sign-in methods, and a self-serve password reset if you forget yours. # Sign in with Google Go to app.vidflow.io. Click Continue with Google . Choose the Google account tied to your Vidflow invite. Your email address must be verified with Google and must match an email that has been granted Vidflow access. See Getting access to Vidflow if you have not been invited yet. # Sign in with email and password Go to app.vidflow.io. Enter your email and password. Click Sign in . If your account has two-factor authentication turned on, you are prompted for a second step after your password is accepted. See Account security for how to set up and manage two-factor authentication. # Resetting your password On the sign-in screen, click Forgot password? . Enter the email address you sign in with and submit the form. Check your inbox for an email with the subject "Reset your Vidflow password." For your privacy, this step always confirms the request went through, whether or not that email has a Vidflow account. Click the reset link in the email. It opens Vidflow directly on the Set a new password screen. Enter a new password and submit. Note Note: The reset link expires one hour after it is sent and can only be used once. Important Important: Setting a new password signs you out everywhere else, on every device and browser where you were previously signed in. You will need to sign back in with the new password on each of them. # Troubleshooting The reset link says it is i

title: What is Vidflow?
description: How Vidflow turns one long-form video into a month of on-brand content, and who it is built for.
body: Vidflow turns one long-form video into clips, captions, and text posts in your brand voice, then schedules everything across your channels, with client approval from one shareable link. # The core idea You upload or import a single long-form video or audio file. Vidflow calls that source an episode. From that one episode, Vidflow can generate short clips, quote and insight posts, blog articles, and images, all written to sound like your brand rather than a generic AI voice. From there you schedule the pieces across your connected accounts or send them to a client for approval first. The goal is one upload in, a full week (or month) of content out. # Who it is for Vidflow is built for video-first creators, podcasters, coaches, and personal brands who record long-form content and need it turned into a steady stream of shorter posts. It also works for agencies running content for multiple client brands at once, since each brand gets its own isolated workspace for content, accounts, voice, and approvals. # Core concepts A few terms come up everywhere in Vidflow: Workspace : one workspace holds one brand's episodes, clips, posts, connected social accounts, brand voice, and approval links. Agencies running several brands create one workspace per brand. See Workspaces and brand spaces . Episode : the long-form source video or audio file you upload or import. Episodes get transcribed automatically, and that transcript powers everything generated from them. Content Library : where eve

title: The Calendar
description: View, schedule, and manage your posts across a month, week, day, or list view.
headings: Facebook and Instagram: know which kind of post you have
body: The Calendar is where you see everything scheduled to publish, drag content onto a date and time, and manage or fix individual posts. # Views Open the Calendar and switch views with the switcher at the top: month , week , day , or list . A Today button jumps back to the current date from anywhere. Month view shows up to 3 posts per day cell, with a "+{n} more" link when a day has more. Week and day views show a time grid from 6 AM to 11 PM in 30-minute slots, with a red line marking the current time on today's column. List view groups posts by day (for example "Today · {Month} {Day}") and shows a red Failed badge on any post with a failed status. An empty day reads "Nothing scheduled yet." # Scheduling content Drag and drop Drag a post from the Unscheduled rail onto a day. Holding it over a day for about half a second opens a Select time popover with hour buttons (for example "9 AM") to pick the exact time. Tap to schedule On touch devices, tap the post instead of dragging. A banner reads "Tap a day/time slot to schedule {title}." with a Cancel option; tap the destination day or time slot to confirm. Either method confirms with a toast: "Scheduled for {Month Day} at {time}." # The Unscheduled rail The right-hand Unscheduled sidebar lists everything with no publish time yet, with a running count. Each row is labeled "{type} · Library" or "{type} · Queue": Library rows are items sitting in the Content Library with no schedule attached. Queue rows are posts already attached to a

title: Post now
description: Publish a piece of content immediately to a connected platform, without scheduling it.
headings: Post a piece of content immediately
