body: Post now sends a single item straight to a connected account, skipping the calendar and queues entirely. # Where to find it Post now is available from: The menu on any card in the Content Library . The Edit content modal for a post or clip. The content detail view, opened from a library card. # Post a piece of content immediately Open Post now from the card menu, the edit modal, or the detail view. Choose a platform from the pills shown. Pills only appear for platforms with a connected account: X, LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok. Review or edit the caption in the text box. A live character counter shows your length against that platform's limit. If TikTok is selected, fill in the TikTok settings panel (see below). Click Post now (or the platform-specific send action) to publish. A successful post shows "Posted to {label}!", with a link to view it when the platform returns one. A failed attempt shows "Publish failed: {message}". Note Note: if no accounts are connected, the dialog shows "No connected accounts. Connect one in Workspace Settings > Connected Accounts." # Platforms and requirements Platform Video required Caption/title cap X No 280 characters LinkedIn No 200 characters (title) Instagram Yes 2,200 characters (caption) Facebook Yes 255 characters (title), 5,000 characters (description) TikTok Yes 150 characters (title) Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok all post a video. If the item you're posting doesn't have one yet, the dialog tells you: "{Platform} posts a

title: Post statuses and failed posts
description: What each post status means, how failures are reported, and how to retry a failed post.
body: Every post in Vidflow carries a status that tells you where it is in the publishing process, plus a separate approval state if approvals apply to it. # Post statuses Status Meaning Draft Not yet scheduled or sent anywhere. Queued Sitting in a queue schedule, waiting for its next open slot. Scheduled Placed on the calendar with a specific date and time. Publishing Actively being sent to one or more platforms right now. Published Successfully sent to at least one platform. Failed Every attempted platform failed. # Approval is a separate dimension A post also has an approval state, independent of the statuses above: needs approval, approved, or rejected. A schedule with Require Approval turned on will not dispatch an item until it reaches approved. See Client approvals overview and Approval status in your library . # What "failed" means Vidflow publishes to every platform an item is set to post on, and it evaluates each platform independently. If every platform attempt fails, the item is marked Failed . If at least one platform succeeds, the item is marked Published , even if others failed. Either way, the specific error for each platform is kept and shown on the item, in the form: instagram: {error}; youtube: {error} So a Published item can still be worth checking: open it to see whether any of its platforms actually failed even though the overall status reads Published. # Retry a failed post Click Retry Post on the item, in the Calendar's Schedule Post dialog or from the conte

title: Schedules and queues
description: Automate posting with recurring timeslots or an ordered queue, with optional tag filters and approval gating.
headings: Recycle Content and Use Optimal Times
Editing, pausing, and deleting a schedule
body: A schedule automates where and when your content publishes, so you don't have to place every post on the calendar by hand. # Create a schedule Open Schedules & Queues . Click Create Schedule . Enter a Schedule Name (for example "Daily Instagram Posts"). Select one or more Platforms for this schedule. Choose a Type : Queue (ordered) or Timeslot (recurring) . Set up the posting schedule (see below) and any filters or toggles you want. Click Create Schedule . An empty Schedules & Queues page reads "No schedules yet" with a prompt to create one. # Queue vs. timeslot Queue (ordered) : content is released one at a time, in order, into the next open slot. Good for a steady drip of a backlog. Timeslot (recurring) : content publishes at specific recurring times you define, for example every Monday and Thursday at 11:00 AM. # Setting times In the posting schedule section, add one or more time rows. Each row has a Time plus day-of-week letter toggles: S M T W R F S, where R stands for Thursday. Click Add another time to add more slots. If the platform supports it, an Optimal Times panel shows suggested time chips for that platform, which you can click to add directly to the schedule. # Feed a schedule with tags Under Filter by Tag , choose one or more tags. Only content carrying at least one of those tags feeds into this schedule automatically. Leave the tag filter empty to manage what's in the schedule manually instead. # Require Approval Turn on Require Approval to hold content until

title: Publish episodes to YouTube and your podcast feed
description: Upload a full episode to YouTube and publish it to your workspace's podcast RSS feed.
body: Full episodes publish to YouTube, and to your podcast feed, from the episode itself rather than from Post now or the Calendar. # Open the Publishing tab Open the episode from the Content Library . Go to its Publishing tab. You'll see two cards: YouTube and your podcast feed. # Upload to YouTube On the YouTube card, choose a privacy setting: Public , Private , or Unlisted . The default is Private . Start the upload. The episode's title, description, and tags feed the upload automatically. Once the upload completes, the card shows a Published badge. Because the default is Private, double-check the privacy dropdown before uploading if you want the video visible right away. # Publish to your podcast feed Your workspace has its own podcast RSS feed, independent of YouTube. On the podcast feed card: Publish to Feed adds the episode to your feed. Once added, the card reads Published to feed . If it hasn't been added yet, it reads Not in podcast feed . Your feed URL follows this pattern: /api/rss/your-workspace-slug It's a standard RSS 2.0 feed with iTunes podcast tags, so it works with Spotify for Podcasters, Apple Podcasts, and most other podcast apps and directories. The feed lists your published episodes, most recent first. Podcast feed settings (like the feed's title and artwork) live in your workspace settings. # Clips are handled separately The Publishing tab only uploads the full episode. To publish a clip to Instagram Reels or Facebook, open the clip itself from the Clips ta

title: Contact support
description: How to reach Vidflow support for product and billing issues, or privacy and data requests.
body: Use the right address for your issue, and include enough detail that support can act on your message right away. # Which address to use support@vidflow.io for product issues, publishing failures, billing questions, refunds, and account deletion. privacy@vidflow.io for privacy questions and data deletion requests. If you are requesting deletion of your data, use the subject line "Data Deletion Request" and send it from the email address on your account. See Your data and privacy . # What to include The more of this you include up front, the faster support can help: Your workspace name. Support needs to know which brand space the issue is on. The specific content item. Link to it in the Content Library, or include its title. What you expected to happen versus what actually happened. Describe both, not just the problem. Timestamps. When you took the action, and when the problem showed up. Screenshots. Especially of any error or failure text shown on screen. If a post failed, copy the exact failure text rather than paraphrasing it. See Fix publishing problems for where that text appears. Note Note: Vidflow does not publish a guaranteed response time. Including the details above is the best way to get a faster answer regardless. # Before you write in A few issues are self-serve and faster to fix directly than to wait on a reply: A failed post is often fixed by reconnecting an account or retrying, see Fix publishing problems . Common questions about access, limits, and platform beh

title: Frequently asked questions
description: Quick answers to the questions customers ask most, with links to the full articles.
headings: How do I get access to Vidflow?
What counts as an AI generation?
Why are my TikTok posts showing as private?
Why are my clips taking a long time?
Do my clients need a Vidflow login to approve content?
Do captions work differently on each platform?
Can I run multiple brands from one account?
What happens when I hit my monthly limit?
Can I cancel and get a refund?
How do I delete my data?
Which platforms publish automatically on a schedule?
Why can't I invite my team?
Why is Flows greyed out or unavailable?
Why does OpusClip say it isn't connected?
body: Quick answers to common questions, each with a link to the full article if you need more detail. # How do I get access to Vidflow? Vidflow is invite-only during the current beta. Join the waitlist from the marketing site, and you will get access in waves. Once you are approved, you receive an email titled "Your Vidflow access is ready" with a link to sign in at app.vidflow.io using that same email address. See Getting access to Vidflow . # What counts as an AI generation? AI generations are a shared monthly pool that includes transcribing an uploaded video, writing clip titles and captions, generating text posts, ideas, scripts, and images, and any other AI-written content. Transcribing a YouTube import does not draw from this pool the same way an upload does, since import quota is tracked separately from the AI pool. Check your plan's allowance and what happens at the cap in Usage limits and monthly allowances . # Why did my post fail? A post can fail for a few reasons: the connected account's authorization expired, the platform's requirements were not met (for example, no video on a platform that requires one, or a caption over the limit), or TikTok rejected the compose settings. The post's failure text tells you which platform failed and why. Work through Fix publishing problems in order, it is built as a checklist from most to least likely cause. # Why are my TikTok posts showing as private? Beta note Beta note: While TikTok's app review is pending, every post from Vidflo

title: Fix publishing problems
description: A checklist for failed posts, ordered by how likely each cause is, with the fix for each one.
headings: 1. Check if the account's authorization died
3. Check for a YouTube quota hold
4. Check for an old X connection missing media permission
5. Check if approval is holding it back
body: When a post fails, work through this checklist in order. It is ordered by how often each cause shows up, so start at the top. # 1. Check if the account's authorization died The most common cause of a failed post is a connected account whose authorization stopped working on the platform's end. When this happens, Vidflow pauses publishing to that account, emails the workspace, and marks it as needing reconnect. The email reads: "Your {platform} connection stopped working, so we've paused publishing to it. This usually happens when {platform} expires the authorization on their end, and it takes about a minute to fix. Reconnect the account and your scheduled posts will pick right back up." Fix it: Go to Workspace Settings > Connections . Find the account with a needs-reconnect status. Reconnect it by signing in again. Once reconnected, anything waiting on that account resumes normally. Note Note: Reconnect timing varies by platform. YouTube and TikTok refresh tokens automatically in the background and only need reconnect when the platform itself revokes access. Meta (Instagram and Facebook) tokens are longer-lived but do not refresh automatically once they expire, so a dead Meta connection needs a manual reconnect. LinkedIn tokens last around 60 days, so expect to reconnect it periodically even without an error. # 2. Check the platform's requirements Some platforms reject posts that do not meet their format requirements, and Vidflow catches most of these before the post goes out.

title: Get the most out of Vidflow by looping in your AI
description: Copy one prompt into ChatGPT or Claude, paste back the result, and watch your brand voice, audience, and image style fill in automatically.
headings: What each field means and how Vidflow uses it
If the paste does not parse
body: Setting up a brand voice from scratch means typing out how you sound, who you talk to, and what your content looks like. Most people already have an AI assistant that knows a lot of that about them already. The AI Shortcut lets that assistant do the typing. # Why loop in your AI If you already talk to ChatGPT, Claude, or another assistant about your business, it likely has a running picture of your brand voice, your audience, and what you post about. Instead of starting from a blank field, you hand it a prompt built for Vidflow, let it ask you a couple of quick questions to fill in any gaps, and bring the answer back. # The one-paste shortcut On the setup wizard, choose Use my AI to fill this out . Click Copy prompt . This copies a ready-made prompt written specifically for Vidflow's brand profile format. Paste the prompt into ChatGPT, Claude, or whichever AI assistant you use. Answer the handful of short questions it asks. If it already knows your business, it may only ask one or two, or skip straight to the answer. Copy the response your AI gives back, in full, including the code block. Paste it into the box on the Vidflow panel. As soon as you paste something, Vidflow reads it and shows a live preview: a checklist of every field it found, with the actual value underneath so you can see exactly what is about to be filled in. Click Fill my profile to apply it. # What each field means and how Vidflow uses it Brand name, industry, and target audience set the basic context ever

title: Set up your brand voice
description: Fill in your brand profile and voice settings so every clip title, caption, and post sounds like you.
body: Everything Vidflow generates, clip titles, captions, posts, scripts, draws on the brand voice settings in your workspace. Filling these in is the one setup step that changes how everything else sounds. # Why this matters Without a brand sheet, target audience, or voice guidance, the AI has nothing to work from and writes in a generic voice. With them filled in, clip titles, captions, and posts start sounding like your brand instead of a template. # Your Brand Profile Open Workspace Settings and go to the Brand tab. The Brand Profile card includes: Brand Name (required). Website , Industry , Location , and Timezone . Target Audience , a free-text field for describing your ideal listener: who they are, what they struggle with, what they want. Campaign Focus Areas , a set of topic chips you add yourself (for example, Leadership or Entrepreneurship) to steer what the AI treats as on-topic. Below that is the Brand Sheet card, where you paste or type a longer brand document, or upload one. This is the single biggest input into how on-brand your generated content sounds, treat it as the one field worth spending real time on. # Your logo The logo you upload with your Brand Profile does more than decorate your own workspace. It also white-labels the client approval portal your clients see when reviewing content, and it appears on your branded analytics reports. Upload a real logo here if you want client-facing surfaces to carry your brand instead of looking generic. # Teaching the AI

title: Invite team members
description: Add teammates to a workspace by email and role, and how client reviewers work differently from working seats.
body: Bring teammates into a workspace so more than one person can work on a brand's content. Note Note: Team access is based on seats. Every workspace includes one working seat, which the owner uses, and each plan includes a set number of working seats, so adding Admin or Brand Manager teammates starts on the Pro plan. Client reviewers never use a seat. On the Free plan the invite controls are locked. See Plans and features . # Send an invite Open Workspace Settings and go to the Team tab. In the Invite a teammate card, enter the person's email address. Choose a role: Admin , Brand Manager , or Client . Click Send invite . The invite appears in a pending list on the same card, with a Revoke option if you need to cancel it before it is accepted. # Roles Admin and Brand Manager are working team members. They count toward your plan's seat limit for this workspace, and the workspace owner always counts as one seat. Client is the reviewer role, meant for people who only need to approve or comment on content. Client seats are always free and never count against your seat limit, no matter how many you add. How many Admin and Brand Manager seats your plan includes varies by plan. See Plans and features for exact seat counts. # How the invitee joins An invited person is added automatically the first time they sign in with the invited email address. If they already have a Vidflow account under that email, they are added to the workspace immediately instead of waiting for a next sign-in. Imp

title: Running multiple brands
description: How agencies run several client brands in Vidflow, one isolated workspace per brand.
body: Agencies running content for more than one client use a separate workspace for each brand. # One workspace per brand Each workspace keeps its content, connected accounts, brand voice, and approval links completely separate from every other workspace. Nothing you set up for one client brand shows up in another, so it is safe to run several client brands from the same Vidflow account. See Workspaces and brand spaces for what a workspace isolates. # Adding more brand spaces Every account includes one workspace on any plan. Running more than one brand requires the Agency plan. Beyond the workspace your plan includes, Agency owners can purchase additional brand spaces as an add-on, added only as you need them. Current pricing for the base plan and for additional brand spaces lives on the Billing page in the app, or on vidflow.io. Note Note: If you try to create a workspace beyond what your plan allows, Vidflow shows a clear in-app message telling you what to do next, either upgrade or add a brand space. # Copying content between workspaces If you have already built out one client's content and want to reuse pieces of it for another brand, you do not need to rebuild from scratch. Bulk Manage includes a Copy Content to Other Workspaces action that copies selected library items into a different workspace, leaving the originals in place. See Bulk actions for the full steps. This is available on plans that include bulk actions. See Plans and features . # Related articles Workspaces and

title: Workspaces and brand spaces
description: How workspaces isolate one brand's content, accounts, voice, and approvals from every other brand.
body: A workspace, also called a brand space, holds one brand's content, clips, and publishing settings. Everything you do in Vidflow happens inside a workspace. # What a workspace isolates Each workspace has its own: Episodes, clips, and Content Library. Brand voice and brand sheet. Connected social accounts. Schedules, queues, and the Calendar. Approval links. Notification settings. Switching to a different workspace switches all of it at once. Nothing from one brand's workspace shows up in another, which is what makes Vidflow safe to use across multiple client brands. # Your first workspace Every account includes one workspace, on every plan, at no cost. If you only manage one brand, you likely never need to think about this again. Running content for more than one brand requires a bigger plan; see Running multiple brands for how that works. # Switching workspaces Fresh sign-ins land on the Your Brand Spaces screen, showing a card for each workspace you belong to. Returning sessions go straight to the last workspace you were working in. Click any card to switch into that workspace. # Creating a workspace From Your Brand Spaces , click to create a new one. In the Create New Brand Space dialog, enter a Brand Name . Confirm the auto-suggested URL slug, or adjust it. Click Create Brand Space . # Renaming a workspace Open Workspace Settings and update the workspace name from the General tab. This is an admin-level action. # Freezing a workspace Freezing is the reversible way to shut
